solution: Compound A is phenol which gives sooty flame on burning. Its reaction with bromine solution in low polarity solvent results in high yield of 4-bromophenol.

solution:
Propanal and methanal undergo aldol condensation reaction with sodium hydroxide. The α,β-unsaturated compound undergo 1,4-conjugated addition with cyanide ion gives a racemic mixture. The cyanide undergo acidic hydrolysis gives carboxylic acid.

Carboxylic acid will give CO2 gas, with NaHCO3 solution

subject: Chemistry
solution: Nitrobenzene is reduced to aniline by passing hydrogen gas in presence of finely divided palladium and also by reduction with metals in an acidic medium. When aniline reacts with acetic anhydride in the presence of pyridine to give acetanilide. Aniline acts as a weak base, while acetic anhydride acts as electrophile.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Products in the given reactions are as follows:

1. Hoffmann bromamide degradation is useful in the production of aniline as it converts the -CONH2 functional group into the -NH2 group. So, we need to take a starting material that has the -CONH2 group and after getting converted into -NH2 group, the molecule obtained is aniline. So, in order to obtain aniline as the final product, we require Benzamide as a starting material. So, in this way, we can easily convert Benzamide to Aniline.

2. Gabriel phthalimide synthesis cannot be used to prepare aniline because aryl halides do not give Gabriel phthalimide reaction.

3. When nitrobenzene is reacted with an active metal like Pd/C and H2,  it produces aniline. This reaction is also called Nitrobenzene reduction.

4. When acetanilide is treated with dil. H2SO4, it produces aniline.

 

Aromatic amines will be formed in 1, 3 & 4.

So, answer is 3.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Amino acids contain amino (-NH2) and carboxyl (-COOH) functional groups. Depending upon the relative position of the amino group with respect to the carboxyl group, the amino acids can be classified as α, β, γ, δ and so on. Only α-amino acids are obtained on the hydrolysis of proteins. 

So, only in option (B) α-amino acid given all the other options are not α-amino acids.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
(A) XeF4:

Xe has 4 bond pairs along with 2 lone pairs in the XeF4. Thus, the hybridisation of Xe is sp3d2. Now since it contains two lone pairs it will show square planar geometry.

(B) SF4:

The lone pair is an equatorial position, and there are two lone-pair—bond pair repulsions. Hence, it is more stable. So, the shape is described as a distorted tetrahedron, a folded square or a see-saw.

(C) NH4+:

In this case steric number = lone pair + sigma bond = 0 + 4 = 4. 

So, it is sp3 hybridisation. There is no lone pair present. Thus, the shape of this ion is tetrahedral.

(D) BrF3:

To decrease repulsion between the lone pairs, the molecule's structure is bent, making it T-shaped.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
For reaction: SO2(g) + 12O2(g) ⇌ SO3(g)

Kp = 2×1012 at 300 K and 1 atm pressure.

The relation between equilibrium constant in terms of pressures Kp and equilibrium constant in terms of concentration Kc is

Kp = Kc × (RT)∆ng

⇒ 2×1012 = Kc × (0.082 × 300)-12

⇒ KC= 9.92 × 1012

⇒ KC = 0.992 × 1013

So, answer is 1.

solution:
If K is known at two different temperatures the activation energy can be calculated as:

At temperature 1: ln K1 = -EaRT1 + ln A

At temperature 2: ln K2 = -EaRT2 + ln A

We can subtract one of these equations from the other:

ln K1 - ln K2 = -EaRT1 + ln A - -EaRT2 + ln A

This equation can further simplified to:

lnK1K2 = EaR1T2 - 1T1 

ln 0.050.03=Ea2.305×8.314×1200-1300

⇒ 0.22 =Ea2.305×8.314×1600

⇒ Ea = 2.3 × 8.3 × 600 × 0.22

⇒Ea=2519.88 J⇒Ea=2520 J

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Saccharin, also called ortho-sulphobenzimide, is the first popular artificial sweetening agent. It is about 550 times as sweet as cane sugar. Its use is of great value to diabetic persons and people who need to control their intake of calories.

Aspartame is the most successful and widely used artificial sweetener. It is roughly 100 times as sweet as cane sugar. It is the methyl ester of dipeptide formed from aspartic acid and phenylalanine. Use of aspartame is limited to cold foods and soft drinks because it is unstable at cooking temperature.

Alitame is a high-potency sweetener, and it is roughly 2000 times as sweet as cane sugar.

Sucralose is trichloro derivative of sucrose. Its appearance and taste are like sugar. It is roughly 600 times as sweet as cane sugar.

So, sweetness value order with respect to cane sugar is:

Alitame > Sucralose > Saccharin > Aspartame

chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:

solution:
Cobalt chloride when dissolved in water forms pink coloured isCoH2O6Cl2 . This is due to the electronic transition of an electron from t2g to eg energy level of CoH2O62+ complex. When excess of HCl is added to the solution CoH2O62+ is transformed into deep blue colour complex [CoCl4]2−.

△t= 49 △o

△t < △o

Tetrahedral complexes have smaller crystal field splitting than octahedral complexes.

CoH2O62+ →Conc. HCl [CoCl4]2-

Hence, X=CoH2O62+, Y=CoCl42-, Z=Tetrahedral

subject: Chemistry
solution:
V2O3: In, V2O3, the oxidation state of V is as follows

2(x) + 3(-2) = 0

⇒ 2x - 6 = 0

⇒ x = 62 = 3

The oxidation state of V in V2O3 is +3. The vanadium is present in its lowest oxidation state. The vanadium tends to donate the pair of electrons. Hence, it is basic oxide.

V2O5: In V2O5, the oxidation state of V is as follows

2(x) + 5(-2) = 0

⇒ 2(x) - 10 = 0

⇒x = 102 = 5

The oxidation state of V in V2O5 is +5. The Vanadium tends to donate and accept the pair of electrons. It is amphoteric oxide.

V2O4: In V2O4, the oxidation state of V is as follows

2(x) + 4(-2) = 0

⇒ 2(x) - 8 = 0

⇒ x = 82 = 4

The oxidation state of V in V2O4 is +4. The Vanadium is present in its other low oxidation state. The Vanadium tends to donate and accept the pair of electrons. It is amphoteric oxide and less basic than V2O3, more basic than V2O5.

So, the correct order of basicity of oxides of vanadium is V2O3>V2O4>V2O5.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The atomic number of Neodymium (Nd) is 60. Its electronic configuration is Xe 4f4 6s2.

Now, by removing two electrons from the outermost shell 6s, Nd2+ ion is formed. Hence, electronic configuration of Nd2+ is Xe 4f4.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Brine solution is the concentrated solution of NaCl in water.

 NaCl (aq.)→Na(aq) ++Cl(aq) +

The cathode will attract the sodium ions from the sodium chloride and hydrogen ions from the water. The hydrogen ions gain 2 electrons and are reduced to form hydrogen molecules.

2H+ (aq) + 2e- → H2 (g)

2H2O + 2e− → H2 (g) + 2OH− (aq)

The anode will attract the hydroxide ions from the water and chlorine ions from sodium chloride. The chlorine ions lose 2 electrons and get oxidised.

Cl2 → 2Cl− + 2e−

Na+ + OH− → NaOH

solution:

Using gibbs free energy equation,

ΔG°=-RT lnK

subject: Chemistry
solution:
A. Dicholoromethane and H2O do not mix & form two separate layers. So they can be separated by Differential solvent extraction.

B. 

Column chromatography is a technique in which the substances to be separated are introduced onto the top of a column packed with an adsorbent, passed through the column at different rates that depend on the affinity of each substance for the adsorbent and for the solvent or solvent mixture, and are usually collected in solution as they pass from the column at different times. Thus, the basic principle involved in column chromatography is to adsorb solutes of the solution with the help of a stationary phase and further separating the mixture into discrete components.

C. Kerosene / Naphthalene: Fractional Distillation, kerosene and naphthalene are different types of hydrocarbons that have different boiling points.

D. C6H12O6/NaCl: C6H12O6/NaCl are different compounds with different chemical and physical properties. As a result, they can be separated from each other by crystallisation.

So, the correct matching is A-(ii), B-(iii), C-(iv), D-(i).

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Electrolysis is not a concentration method. Electrolytic refining is a process of refining a metal (mainly copper) by the process of electrolysis.

Liquation is not a concentration method. It is a refining method. In this method a low melting metal like tin can be made to flow on a sloping surface. In this way it is separated from higher melting impurities.

The other three methods are concentration methods of ores.

Leaching is used when the ore is soluble in a solvent. The powdered ore is dissolved in a chemical, usually a strong solution of NaOH. The chemical solution dissolves the metal in the ore, and it can be extracted and separated from the gangue by extracting the chemical solution.

Froth flotation is a process for selectively separating hydrophobic materials from hydrophilic.

Hydraulic washing is a technique used when the impurities are lighter and the ore particles are heavier. The lighter impurities are removed by washing in current of water. As gold particles are heavier than the impurities like sand, we can use hydraulic washing for the concentration of ores of gold.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Out of the three isomers of dichlorobenzene, the para-isomer is more symmetrical than the other two isomers, So, it has a more closely packed arrangement of molecules in its crystal lattice. So, para-dichlorobenzene has a higher melting point as compared to ortho and meta Isomers.

Melting point of ortho-dichlorobenzene, meta-dichlorobenzene and para-dichlorobenzene are 256K, 249K, 323K respectively.

Bromo, iodo and polychloro derivatives of hydrocarbons are heavier than water. The density increases with increase in number of carbon atoms, halogen atoms and atomic mass of the halogen atoms.

So, the correct option is D.

solution:
Here, the H2O2 act as the reducing agent, as the Cl has +1 oxidation state of in NaOCl and after reaction with the H2O2, in the presence of the acid, it reduces the Cl in NaOCl to Cl- having -1 oxidation state.

So, as Cl oxidation state decreases from the +1 to -1 oxidation state, hence the H2O2 is acting as the reducing agent.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In the summer, nitrogen dioxide and methane react with chlorine monoxide and chlorine atoms which results in a shrinkage of chlorine and hence prevents ozone layer depletion.

ClO (g) ∙+NO2 (g) ⟶ClON(X)O2 (g)

Cl (g) + CH4 (g) → CH3 (g) + HCl (g)

During winter, special types of clouds are formed over the Antarctic region. These clouds provide the surface for the hydrolysis of chlorine nitrate to form hypochlorous acid. 

ClONO2 (g) + H2O (g) → HOCl (g) + HNO3 (g)                                                  (Y)                 (Z)

So, X=ClONO2, Y=HOCl, Z=HNO3

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The reaction between Cu2+ and KI will takes place as:

Cu2++2KI⟶CuI2↓Unstable+2 K+

I- is a strong reducing agent, it reduces Cu2+ to Cu+ and a white precipitate of Cu2I2 is formed.

2CuI2⟶Cu2I2↓(White)'X'+I2

Sodium thiosulphate is used as reducing agent to titrate iodine, with iodide being the product of the reaction.

2Na2 S2O3 + I2 → 2NaI+ Na2 S4O6(Y)

So, the compound X and Y are Cu2I2 and Na2S4O6 respectively.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Micelles are formed by self-assembly of amphiphilic molecules. The structures contain hydrophilic/polar region (head) and hydrophobic/nonpolar region (tail). Micelles are formed in aqueous solution whereby the polar region faces the outside surface of the micelle and the nonpolar region forms the core.

Non-polar part will interact with non-polar solvent.

subject: Mathematics
options: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>7</mn></math>, then the system has no solution

If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>β</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>7</mn></math> then the system has a unique solution.

There is a unique point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>)</mo></math> on the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>18</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> for which the system has infinitely many solutions

For every point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>≠</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>)</mo></math> on the line <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, the system has infinitely many solutions.
solution:
Given system of linear equations:

x+y+z=6

αx+βy+7z=3

x+2y+3z=14

On taking determinant, we get

A=111αβ7123

=(3β-14)-(3α-7)+(2α-β)

=2β-α-7

Here, if α=β=7 then A=0 and the system has no solution. So, option A is correct.

If α=β, α≠7 then the system has a unique solution. So, option B is correct.

If (α, β)≠(7, 7) then A≠0. So, system has not infinitely many solution. Therefore, option D is false.

y1=sin3π3cos2π3=-18

Therefore,

2y'1+3π2y1=0

For maximum distance, denominator must be minimum.

4sec2θ+b2cosec2θ

=4+4tan2θ+b2+b2cot2θ

4tan2θ+b2cot2θ2≥4tan2θ×b2cot2θ

⇒4+b2+4tan2θ+b2cot2θ≥2+b2

So, minimum value of 4sec2θ+b2cosec2θ is 2+b2=b+2

So,

d=4-b22+b=1

⇒2-b=1

⇒2-b=±1

⇒b=1, 3

But b<2, so b=1, hence

e=1-14=32

solution:
Number of five-digit numbers formed using the digits 0, 2, 3, 4, 7, 9 with 2 at the first place is

=6×6×6×6=1296

Since, 2+6+6+6+6=1296

Number of five-digit numbers formed using the given digits with 3 at the first place is

=1296

Since, 3+6+6+6+6=1296

Number of five-digit numbers formed using the given digits with  40 at the first place is

=216

Since, 40+6+6+6=216

And,

420+6+6=36

4 2 2+6+6=36

423+6+6=36

424+6+6=36

427+6+6=36

429 0+6=6

429 2 0=1

429 2 2=1

429 2 3=1

Required serial number is

=1296×2+216+5×36+6+1+1+1=2997

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

x2-4x+3+x2-9=4x2-14x+6

⇒x-3x-1+x-3x+3=x-34x-2

⇒x-3x-1+x-3x+3-x-34x-2=0

⇒x-3=0 or x-1+x+3-4x-2=0

So, x=3 is one solution and also x≥3

Now solving x-1+x+3-4x-2=0

⇒x-1+x+3=4x-2

Now squaring both side we get,

⇒x-1+x+3+2x-1x+3=4x-2

⇒2x-1x+3=2x-4

⇒x-1x+3=x-22

⇒x2+2x-3=x2-4x+4

⇒6x=7⇒x=76 which does not satisfy the given expression and x≥3

So, there is only one solution which is x=3

a,ar,ar2,ar3a,r>0

Let r1/2+r-1/2=A

=A3-3A

Therefore,

A3-3A+A=21

⇒A3-2A=21

⇒A=3

∴ r+1r=3

⇒r+1=3r

On squaring both sides:

r2+2r+1=9r

⇒r2-7r+1=0

Here, sum of roots=7

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Four-digit numbers that are less than or equal to 2800 are 1000,1001,1002,....,2800

Numbers divisible by 3 are 1002, 1005, ....,2796,2799

Let total numbers be n, then

1002+n-1 3=2799 

⇒n=600

Numbers divisible by 11 are 1001, 1012, ..., 2794

Let total numbers be m, then

2794=1001+m-111

⇒m=164

Numbers divisible by 33 are

1023,1056,...,2772

Let total numbers be r, then

2772=1023+r-133

⇒r=54

Number of 4-digit numbers that are less than or equal to 2800 and either divisible by 3 or by 11 , is equal to

=600+164-54=710

subject: Mathematics
options: symmetric but neither reflexive nor transitive
transitive but neither reflexive nor symmetric
reflexive and symmetric but not transitive
symmetric and transitive but not reflexive
solution:
Given, a,bRc,d⇒adb-c=bca-d

⇒a,bRc,d⇔b-cbc=a-dad

⇒1c-1b=1d-1a

⇒1a-1b=1d-1c

Reflexive:

a,bRa,b⇒1a-1b=1b-1a

It is false. So, given relation is not reflexive.

Symmetric:

a,bRc,d⇒1a-1b=1d-1c

⇒1c-1d=1b-1a

∴c,dRa,b

It is symmetric.

Transitive:

a,bRc,d⇒1a-1b=1d-1c

c,dRe,f=1c-1d=1e-1f

1a-1b=1e-1f

⇒a,bRe,f

So, not transitive.

solution:
Given,

a→+b→+c→=a→+b→-c→

Now squaring both side we get,

⇒a→+b→+c→2=a→+b→-c→2

⇒a→2+b→2+c→2+2a→·b→+2b→·c→+2c→·a→=a→2+b→2+c→2+2a→·b→-2b→·c→-2c→·a→

⇒2b→·c→+2c→·a→=-2b→·c→+2c→·a→

⇒4b→·c→+c→·a→=0

Now here given b→·c→=0, so

c→·a→=0 which means c→ & a→ are perpendicular vector, so statement B is false,

Now statementA: a→+λc→≥a→

⇒a→+λc→2≥a→2

⇒a→2+λc→2+2a→·c→≥a→2

⇒λc→2+2a→·c→≥0

⇒λc→2≥0

This is not always true, since λ∈R.

Hence, we can say that statementA is true and statement B is false.

fc=100π2π=500 Hz

Modulating wave frequency is 

fm=4π2π=2 Hz

subject: Physics
solution:
Drift velocity of electrons through a conductor kept in an external electric field is given by Vd=-eEmτ=-eVmlτ 

Where, e=magnitude of charge on electron, m=mass of electron, E=external electric field and τ=average relaxation time.

As, drift velocity is independent of area. So, the new drift velocity of electrons will be Vd.

solution:
Let r be the internal resistance of each cell.

In above shown figure, first represents the series connection of cells. Second and third figures represents the parallel connection of cells.

Now, in case of series connection, εnet=2ε and rnet=2r. So, current i=2ε5+2r .....(1)

In case of parallel connection, εnet=εr+εrr+r=ε and rnet=r2. So, current i=εr2+5 .....(2)

Equating (1) and (2), we get

2ε5+2r=εr2+5

⇒r+10=5+2r

⇒r=5 Ω.

solution:
The magnitude of acceleration due to gravity g1 at a depth of d below the surface of Earth is

g1=GMR3R-d=GMR21-dR    ....(1)

The magnitude of acceleration due to gravity g2 at a height d above the surface of Earth is

g2=GMR+d2    ....(2)

Given here, g1=4g2

GMR21-dR=4GMR+3R2

⇒GMR21-dR=4GM4R2

⇒1-dR=14⇒dR=34⇒d=34R

⇒d=4800 km

subject: Physics
options: Time taken by them to reach the earth's surface will be independent of the properties of their materials
Insulating ball will reach the earth's surface earlier than the metal ball
Both will reach the earth's surface simultaneously
Metal ball will reach the earth's surface earlier than the insulating ball
solution: When a spherical insulating ball and a spherical metallic ball of same size and mass are dropped from the same height, then magnetic field will act on metallic ball. If the earth's magnetic field is taken into account, then an induced eddy current will form inside the spherical metallic ball, thus retarding its motion. Hence, it will take more time than insulating ball.

solution:
For a bar magnet, the work done in turning from parallel to antiparallel position relative to the field direction is 

W=MBcosθ1-cosθ2, here, θ1=0° and θ1=180°.

W=-MBcos180°-mBcos0°

W=2 MB=2×5×0.4=4 J

43πR3=100043π13

⇒R=10 mm

Final potential energy:

Uf=10004πR2T=1000×4π10×10-32T

Initial potential energy:

Ui=4π×r2T=4π×10-32T

Therefore, change in potential energy,

∆E=7.92×10-4J
